predictions.description: where dept='eng' AND score>5 keeps Alice+Cara (numeric comparison, not string), order by score ascending puts Cara(9) before Alice(42), and matched rows are the ORIGINAL objects — Alice's nested {x:1} survives even though the DataTable view used for filtering flattens it.
Empty where + 'score DESC' keeps all three rows and sorts 42 > 17 > 9.
json-to-csv defaults: comma delimiter, header row, columns are the UNION of keys across ragged objects (a,b,c) with blank cells where a row lacks a key, \n line endings, no trailing newline.
delimiter keyword 'semicolon' maps to ';' and includeHeaders=false drops the header row (column union still applies).
json-to-csv on an empty array PASSES and stores an empty string — the SENTINEL seed proves the write happened.
json-to-xml (pretty=false): '@_' -prefixed keys become attributes, '#text' becomes element text, an array value repeats its element — exact single-line output.
